page: The Vultr vx1-m-16c-128gb is a memory-optimized virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. Each of the 16 physical cores operates at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz with a single thread per core under a shared CPU allocation model. The processor includes a cache hierarchy consisting of 48 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache, alongside support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and SHA_NI.

This server is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage is limited to a 1 GB network-backed volume, indicating that primary storage requirements must be met via external network-attached storage volumes. The network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU metrics, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all placing in the top 10% of tested servers. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average tier. This combination of high memory capacity and strong single-core execution makes the server suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated multi-core CPU utilization, such as in-memory caching, medium-sized database hosting, and lightweight LLM text generation.
description: The Vultr vx1-m-16c-128gb is a memory-optimized virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor on the x86_64 architecture, it operates at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation. The hardware profile provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, backed by a 32 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512. While local storage is limited to a 1 GB network volume, the server excels in single-core benchmarks, registering top-tier performance in single-threaded compression and single-core stress tests. Multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth perform at average levels. This configuration is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-heavy applications, making it well-suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and text generation tasks that benefit from large memory footprints without requiring dedicated CPU cores.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-16c-128gb is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and 1 GB of network storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-16c-128gb is a memory-optimized server with 16 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server combining a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio with strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
1 GB network-attached local storage and one public IPv4 address
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and text generation
